title: Examining the Vision Object's Multiple Results
description:
Examining the Vision Object's Multiple Results

Examining the Vision Object's Multiple Results

If you look closely at the Results list heading you can see where it says something like "1 of 10". (Assume for the sake of this discussion that we had set the NumberToFind property to 10 for a Blob object.)  This means the Current result is 1 out of 10 features which were searched for (as defined by the NumToFind property.)  It is important to remember that the 2nd number in the Results list heading is the value of the NumToFind property and not the number of features that were actually found.  This is done because maybe you want to find 10 features but only found 5.  You will probably want to know why only 5 features were found, so results are calculated and can be displayed the top 10 features found.  This gives you a method to see why the other 5 features were not found.

You can examine the results for any of the multiple blobs you tried to find by changing the CurrentResult property to reflect the blob you would like to see.  This can be done by manually typing a number into the value field of the CurrentResult property or by moving the cursor to the value field of the CurrentResult property and then using the SHIFT+DnArrow or SHIFT+UpArrow keys to move through the results.  You will see the results displayed in the Results list. You can always tell which result is displayed in the Results list by either looking at the Current Result property or by just looking at the heading of the Results list. It will display "Result 1 of 10" for the 1st result when NumberToFind is set to 10 and "Result 2 of 10" for the 2nd result, and "Result 3 of 10" for the 3rd result, etc.

Caution: Be careful with this, as you may end up getting the wrong results because the CurrentResult was set wrong for your SPEL program.  For example, if a SPEL+ program uses VGET to get the RobotXYU result from a vision object, and the CurrentResult is set to 3, then the VGET instruction will return the RobotXYU position for the 3rd result of the multiple-result object.  When the CurrentResult property is changed, this also changes which result is returned to SPEL+ from a program which does a VGET to get any of the results.

Suggestion: Always set the CurrentResult property in your SPEL+ program when working with multiple results.
